title: Xanthosoma aristeguietae
taxonomy: Xanthosoma aristeguietae is a species of flowering plant in the family Arumaceae, commonly known as the elephant ear or calla lily family. It belongs to the genus Xanthosoma, which is a group of tropical plants native to the Americas, particularly in the Andean region. The genus Xanthosoma is further classified as part of the subfamily Monsteroideae, which includes other genera such as Monstera and Philodendron. Xanthosoma aristeguietae is a type of aroid, characterized by its large, heart-shaped leaves and tuberous rhizomes. As a member of the Arumaceae family, Xanthosoma aristeguietae shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as the production of inflorescences and the presence of toxic compounds like calcium oxalate crystals.
